steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The prompt says the student wants to contrast the two samples. Contrast means showing a difference between them — not what they share, and not a general fact about torsion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'When the fibers were twisted as part of the 2019 study, the surface temperature of both samples increased.': Describes what both samples have in common (both heated up). No difference is stated. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Twisting an object will generally cause its temperature to increase, a process known as torsional heating.': A general definition of torsional heating with no mention of either sample specifically. ✗
  • Choice C — 'In 2019, researchers studied the effect of torsional heating on various fibers, including samples of four-ply NiTi wire and three-ply spandex rubber.': Names both samples but only says they were studied — no difference between them is identified. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The four-ply NiTi wire sample used in the 2019 study was thicker than the three-ply spandex rubber sample.': Directly compares the two samples on a measurable dimension (thickness: 0.7 mm vs. 0.2 mm), which is a concrete contrast. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
